instructions: The figures in the right-hand margin indicate marks.
Use of scientific calculator is permitted.

sections.title: Section A
sections.header_notes: Attempt all questions in this section.

subquestions.text: A mechanical system is modeled by the differential equation y'' + 4y = f(x). If f(x) = sec(2x), analyze why the Method of Undetermined Coefficients cannot be applied to find the particular integral and suggest an alternative approach.

In a control system circuit, the input voltage is represented by a unit step function u(t-3). Determine the Laplace transform of the rate of change of current, given that the initial current in the circuit is zero.
subquestions.topic: Laplace transform of derivates and integrals

subquestions.text: For the power series sum of [(n!)^2 / (2n)!] * x^n, evaluate the specific boundary values of 'x' where the D’Alembert’s Ratio Test fails to provide a conclusion regarding convergence.

A two-dimensional fluid flow is described by a velocity potential u(x,y). If the stream function is v(x,y) = e^x sin(y), construct the corresponding velocity potential such that the flow remains irrotational.
Examine the singularity of the complex function f(z) = (1 - cos z) / z^2 at the origin. Determine if this singularity can be removed and justify your reasoning based on the limit behavior.

Consider the variable coefficient equation x^2 y'' - xy' + y = ln(x). Formulate the substitution required to transform this into a linear differential equation with constant coefficients and state the resulting characteristic equation.

A signal f(t) has a Laplace transform F(s). If the signal is time-compressed such that we observe f(2t), demonstrate using the change of scale property how the frequency spectrum is shifted in the s-domain.

sections.title: Section B
sections.header_notes: Solve all questions. Show detailed steps for full credit.

subquestions.text: A periodic voltage signal is defined by f(x) = x^2 in the interval (-pi, pi). By determining the Fourier coefficients, apply Parseval’s Identity to find the exact sum of the infinite series 1/1^4 + 1/2^4 + 1/3^4 + ...

Determine the values of constants 'a' and 'b' such that the function u = ax^3 + bxy^2 is harmonic. Once determined, find its harmonic conjugate 'v' and express the complete analytic function f(z) in terms of the complex variable 'z'.

Evaluate the complex line integral of f(z) = exp(2z) / (z + 1)^4 along the closed contour C defined by the circle |z| = 2. Utilize the Cauchy Integral Formula for higher-order derivatives to obtain the result.

Two coupled mechanical oscillators are governed by the simultaneous equations: dx/dt + 2x - 3y = t and dy/dt - 3x + 2y = exp(2t). Solve this system for x(t) and y(t) assuming the system starts from rest at the origin.

The transfer function of a signal processing unit is H(s) = s / (s^2 + a^2)^2. Employ the Convolution Theorem to find the inverse Laplace transform, effectively determining the unit impulse response of the system in the time domain.

sections.title: Section C
sections.header_notes: Attempt any ONE part from each question.
sections.attempt_rule: Attempt any one part from each question

options.label: a
options.text: Investigate the convergence of the infinite series whose n-th term is given by u_n = [sqrt(n+1) - sqrt(n)] / n^p. Determine the range of values for 'p' for which the series is absolutely convergent.

options.label: b
options.text: A heat conducting rod of length L=1 is initially at a temperature profile f(x) = exp(x). To analyze the cooling process, expand this function into a half-range Fourier sine series valid for the interval (0, 1).
options.topic: Half range Fourier sine and cosine series

options.label: a
options.text: Find the unique Mobius transformation that maps the points z1=0, z2=1, z3=infinity in the z-plane onto the points w1=-1, w2=-i, w3=1 in the w-plane. Verify the transformation for an arbitrary point.

options.label: b
options.text: Analyze the conformal mapping defined by w = z + 1/z. Identify the critical points where the mapping ceases to be conformal and describe the image of the unit circle |z|=1 under this transformation.

options.label: a
options.text: Develop the Laurent series expansion for the function f(z) = 1 / [(z-1)(z-2)] that is valid specifically in the annular region defined by 1 < |z| < 2.

options.label: b
options.text: Evaluate the real trigonometric integral from 0 to 2*pi of [1 / (5 + 4 cos theta)] d(theta) by converting it into a contour integral around the unit circle and applying the Residue Theorem.
options.topic: Cauchy’s Residue theorem and its application

options.label: a
options.text: Solve the non-homogeneous differential equation y'' - 2y' + y = exp(x) * ln(x) using the Method of Variation of Parameters, ensuring the determination of both the complementary function and the particular integral.

options.label: b
options.text: A structural beam deflection is modeled by (1+x)^2 y'' + (1+x) y' + y = 4 cos(ln(1+x)). Solve this second-order linear differential equation with variable coefficients for the general solution.
options.topic: Second order linear differential equations with variable coefficients

options.label: a
options.text: Derive the Laplace transform of a periodic square wave function f(t) having a period of 2T, where f(t) = k for 0 < t < T and f(t) = -k for T < t < 2T.

options.label: b
options.text: Use the Laplace transform method to solve the initial value problem y'' + 9y = cos(2t), given the initial conditions y(0) = 1 and y'(0) = 0. Explain the physical significance of the resulting terms.
options.topic: Application of Laplace Transform to solve ordinary differential equations and simultaneous differential equations
